title: Add amenities field to hostels with display and edit support

requirements.summary: Display amenities in HostelCard with compact badge pills showing up to 5 amenities and a '+N more' indicator
acceptanceCriteria: HostelCard shows up to 5 amenity pills below the price/sharing section.
Each pill is styled as a small badge using the existing warm color palette.
If more than 5 amenities exist, a '+N more' badge is displayed.
If a hostel has no amenities, the amenities row is not rendered.
The card layout does not break on mobile or desktop.

file_operations.description: Add amenities display section below the price and sharing details. Render up to 5 amenity badges using the warm amber/sage color palette. Include logic to show a '+N more' indicator when more than 5 amenities exist. Only render the amenities row when the hostel has at least one amenity. Ensure responsive layout on mobile and desktop.

requirements.summary: Add dedicated Amenities section to HostelDetail page showing all amenities with icons and labels
acceptanceCriteria: HostelDetail page has a clearly labelled 'Amenities' section.
All amenities for the hostel are displayed as icon+label pills.
The section is placed between room sharing/pricing and the map.
If a hostel has no amenities, the section is hidden or shows 'No amenities listed'.
The section is fully responsive on both mobile and desktop.

file_operations.description: Create a reusable AmenitiesSection component that accepts an amenities array prop and renders all amenities as icon+label pills. Map each amenity to an appropriate icon (WiFi, snowflake for AC, fork-and-knife for Meals, car for Parking, dumbbell for Gym, etc.). Style the pills using the warm terracotta/sage/amber color palette. Include conditional rendering to hide the section or show 'No amenities listed' when the array is empty. Ensure responsive grid layout for mobile and desktop.

Import and integrate the AmenitiesSection component. Place the amenities section between the room sharing/pricing section and the map section. Pass the hostel's amenities array from the backend query response to the component. Ensure consistent styling with the existing page layout.

requirements.summary: Add amenities checklist editing to EditHostel page with pre-populated values and form submission
acceptanceCriteria: EditHostel page includes a checklist of at least 12 common amenities.
Checkboxes are pre-populated with the hostel's current amenities when the page loads.
Users can check and uncheck amenities freely.
On form submission, the updated amenities array is sent to the backend updateHostel function.
Success toast is shown after saving and the user is returned to the hostel detail page.
The amenities section is styled consistently with the rest of the EditHostel form.

file_operations.description: Add an amenities editing section to the form with a checklist of common amenities: WiFi, AC, Laundry, Parking, Meals, Gym, Hot Water, Power Backup, Security, CCTV, Study Room, Common Kitchen. Add state management for the selected amenities array. Pre-populate the checkboxes with the hostel's current amenities from the fetched data. Update the form submission handler to include the amenities array in the UpdateHostelInput payload. Ensure consistent styling with the existing form layout using the warm color palette.

requirements.summary: Update useUpdateHostel mutation hook to include amenities field in the update payload
acceptanceCriteria: The useUpdateHostel mutation hook includes amenities in the update payload.
The hook compiles without TypeScript errors.
Calling the hook with an updated amenities array correctly updates the hostel in the backend.

file_operations.description: Update the useUpdateHostel mutation to accept and pass the amenities field in the UpdateHostelInput payload sent to the backend updateHostel function. Ensure the mutation signature matches the backend interface type definition for UpdateHostelInput which includes amenities as Array<string>. Verify TypeScript compatibility with the backend.d.ts interface.
